results.title: What is the AI Life Cycle? - Data Science PM
excerpts:
Section Title: What is the AI Life Cycle? > Understanding the AI Life Cycle > Deployment
Content:
Once the model is performing satisfactorily, it is deployed to a production environment where it can start solving real-world problems. Deployment might involve integrating the model with existing systems, creating an application or service that uses the model, or leveraging the insights via an offline context such as a report to management.
 ... 
Section Title: What is the AI Life Cycle? > Key Take-aways
Content:
Using a well-defined AI project life cycle should not be optional—it should be an integral part of successful AI development. Embracing a life cycle approach can significantly improve the efficiency, productivity, and overall success of AI projects, making it an essential consideration for any team venturing into the world of AI.
For more information on AI project management, explore our post on [6 Concepts to Help Lead an AI Team](https://www.datascience-pm.com/manage-ai-projects/) .
Explore Related Content

results.title: Collaborate with Gemini in Google Drive (Workspace Experiments) - Computer - Google Drive Help
excerpts:
Section Title: Collaborate with Gemini in Google Drive (Workspace Experiments)
Content:
**Feature availability**
This feature is available as part of our early access testing program, Google Workspace Experiments .
To turn off Google Workspace Experiments features, exit the Workspace Experiments program .
This feature is also available through an eligible Google Workspace or Google AI plan. Learn about Gemini features and plans .
Section Title: Collaborate with Gemini in Google Drive (Workspace Experiments) > Supported languages
Content:
You can use the Gemini in Drive side panel in some languages. Learn more about language availability .
Computer Android iPhone & iPad
More
More
More
With Gemini in Google Drive, you can:
Summarize a long document or an entire folder so you can quickly get the main points.
Discuss a topic that requires synthesizing information from one or more files in your Drive, either across your files or within a specific folder.
Retrieve quick facts about a project or topic without the need to find and read the relevant documents.
Move or create Google files and folders.
Section Title: Collaborate with Gemini in Google Drive (Workspace Experiments) > Get started with Gemini in Drive
Content:
On your computer, go to [drive.google.com](https://drive.google.com/) .
At the top right, click Ask Gemini.
In the side panel, select a suggested prompt or write your own prompt.Select a suggestionEnter a prompt in Gemini
At the bottom, in the prompt box, replace the example text with the information that you want.
Press **Enter** .
Press **Enter** .
Optional: To remove your history, click More options **Clear history** .
**Important:** You lose your conversation history when:
You reload your browser.
You close and reopen the document.
Your computer goes offline.
 ... 
Section Title: Collaborate with Gemini in Google Drive (Workspace Experiments) > Explore Gemini in Drive
Content:
| **Action** | **Description** |
| Ask Gemini | Open Gemini. |
| More options | Clear your recent Gemini history and ask for more suggestions. |
| Expand | Make Gemini larger. |
| Collapse | Return Gemini to its original size. |
| Gem | To save time with repetitive tasks, chat with a Gem or create your own. |
| Export | Export the response from Gemini to a new Google Doc or Sheet. |
| Clear selected sources | Clear the files or folders you previously selected to reset Gemini's scope. |
Section Title: Collaborate with Gemini in Google Drive (Workspace Experiments) > Search for your files
Content:
On your computer, open [Google Drive](https://drive.google.com) .
At the top right, click Ask Gemini.
To find a file based on its contents, in the prompt, enter the file details.
 ... 
Section Title: Collaborate with Gemini in Google Drive (Workspace Experiments) > Give feedback on generated output
Content:
Gemini for Google Workspace is constantly learning and may not be able to support your request. Because feedback may be human readable, please do not submit data that contains personal, confidential, or sensitive information.
If you get a suggestion that’s inaccurate or that you feel is unsafe, you can submit feedback. Your feedback can help improve AI-assisted Workspace features and broader Google efforts in AI.
At the bottom right of the generated text, click Good suggestionor Bad suggestion.
If you select Bad suggestion, you can select the issue you found and enter additional feedback.
**Optional:**
To review data that’ll be attached with your feedback, at the bottom, select the data.
If you don’t want to include the data with your feedback, to help us improve the product experience, uncheck the box next to “Attach collected data to your feedback.”
 ... 
Section Title: Collaborate with Gemini in Google Drive (Workspace Experiments) > Related resources
Content:

results.title: File storage and Library in ChatGPT  | OpenAI Help Center
excerpts:
All Collections
ChatGPT
File storage and Library in ChatGPT
Section Title: File storage and Library in ChatGPT
Content:
Updated: yesterday
Library is currently only available to ChatGPT Plus, Pro, and Business users outside of the outside of the European Economic Area, Switzerland, and the United Kingdom.
The files you upload to and create in ChatGPT are now saved to your [**Library**](https://chatgpt.com/library) , so you can find and reuse them later. Library is currently only available on web.
ChatGPT automatically saves uploaded and created files, including files uploaded in chats (for example: documents, spreadsheets, presentations, and images) in a dedicated, secure location so they can be easily accessed later. Generated images will continue to appear in the [Images](https://chatgpt.com/images/) tab.
 ... 
Section Title: File storage and Library in ChatGPT > **What files are saved?**
Content:
ChatGPT automatically saves uploaded and created files, including files uploaded in chats (for example: documents, spreadsheets, presentations, and images) in a dedicated, secure location so they can be easily accessed later.
Generated images will continue to appear in the [Images](https://chatgpt.com/images/) tab.
 ... 
Section Title: File storage and Library in ChatGPT > **How will OpenAI use my saved files and chats?**
Content:
OpenAI uses your files and chats according to your settings and data controls. For example, if you have turned on [Memory](https://help.openai.com/en/articles/8590148-memory-faq) , files and chats may be used to help ChatGPT remember useful details about you between chats.
 ...

results.title: Chat and File Retention Policies in ChatGPT | OpenAI Help Center
excerpts:
All Collections
ChatGPT
File uploads
Chat and File Retention Policies in ChatGPT
 ... 
Section Title: **Files**
Content:
Files uploaded during a conversation (e.g., documents, images) are tied to that conversation's lifecycle.
If you delete the conversation that contained an uploaded file:
The file (if still active) is immediately scheduled for deletion alongside the chat.
The 30-day maximum deletion timeline applies, unless the file had already expired naturally
 ...

results.title: OpenAI rolls out ChatGPT Library to store your personal files
excerpts:
Section Title: OpenAI rolls out ChatGPT Library to store your personal files
Content:
By
 ... 
Section Title: ... > [Mayank Parmar](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/author/mayank-parmar/)
Content:
"ChatGPT automatically saves uploaded and created files, including files uploaded in chats (for example: documents, spreadsheets, presentations, and images) in a dedicated, secure location so they can be easily accessed later," OpenAI [noted](https://help.openai.com/en/articles/20001052-file-storage-and-library-in-chatgpt) in a document.
On the other hand, if you use ChatGPT to generate AI images, they will continue to appear in the Images tab.
The Library section only has files you uploaded, and you can upload files by following these steps:
Open the composer menu (the attachment/add button).
Select Add from library.
Choose the file you want to use.
OpenAI says ChatGPT automatically saves uploaded and created files, including files uploaded in chats, in a dedicated, secure location so they can be easily accessed later.

results.title: Persistent Memory & Context Issues with ChatGPT-4 Despite Extensive Prompting - ChatGPT - OpenAI Developer Community
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > post by Foxalabs on Dec 11, 2024
Content:
Foxalabs Leader
Dec 2024
Hi, you can manually edit the memory ChatGPT has about you, you should put important information in there.
Each time you speak with ChatGTP it’s the first time, ChatGPT has no momeory other than the items that get sent to the model each time you speak with it, those are included in the memory feature.
The model is not self learning and will not retain information accross chats or even for vert long chats, there is a contextual limit to the number of words (actually tokens) that the AI can process each time you send a new question, so older information will be lost from the top of the list.
The chat experience is an illusion created by sending all of the conversation thus far to the model each time and just showingf you the latest reply.

results.title: ChatGPT Library Gives Paid Users Persistent File Storage
excerpts: 4 days ago — OpenAI has launched ChatGPT Library , a persistent file storage hub for paid subscribers that saves uploaded documents across chat sessions.
